assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response explicitly explains dual eviction risk - both planned eviction from upgrade process and unplanned eviction from spot preemption, noting 30-second notice for spot preemption

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response mentions that stateless applications handle spot eviction well during upgrades and provides workload-specific guidance based on fault tolerance

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends conservative surge settings (maxSurge=1) for spot pools, contrary to this expectation which suggests higher maxSurge

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response consistently recommends maxUnavailable=0 for spot pools in the conservative approach, not >0 as expected

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly addresses this concern, noting that spot VMs may not be available in the same zone during surge upgrades and provides troubleshooting for when surge nodes won't provision

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions PDBs multiple times, including setting PDB maxUnavailable: 25% for stateless applications and strict PDBs for stateful applications

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple gcloud commands for configuring surge settings, including specific examples for updating max-surge-upgrade and max-unavailable-upgrade

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response recommends the opposite - upgrading on-demand pools first, then spot pools second, stating this in the recommendation summary
